title: Mary, and the Voice That Knew Her
subtitle: The risen Lord speaks one word — her name — and turns her grief into joy

stillness: Be still, and do not yet reach out your hands.
Let your soul be where your Master is.
Wait for the voice that knows your name.
gospel: Turning, she says to him: Rabboni — that is, Master.
Jesus says to her: Do not touch me,
for I have not yet ascended to my Father.
Go to my brethren and say to them:
I ascend to my Father and your Father,
to my God and your God.
Mary came, announcing: I have seen the Lord,
and these things he said to me.
behold: Behold, here is a wondrous working of love.
She had heard from one angel that he was risen,
and afterward from two that he was alive;
and she does not remember, and she says: I do not know.
This love was doing — for her soul was not where she herself was,
but there, where her Master was.
Her spirit was more in the body of her Master than in her own.
She knew not how to think, or speak, or hear, except of him.
See her, while she weeps, looked upon with so sweet an eye,
and called with so gentle a voice: Mary.
At this voice all the floodgates of her head are broken open;
tears are drawn out from the very marrow,
sobs and sighs are dragged up from the deepest inward parts.
She runs and falls down at the feet of Jesus,
to adore his footprints, to touch and kiss his feet, as she was wont.
consider: He had called her by the common word of her sex, and was not recognized;
so he calls her by her own name, to make her more intent,
and shows himself such, that she might know him.
As if he said openly to her: Know him again, by whom you are known again.
For since with God the number of the elect is certain,
great grace is given to some, when they are shown by their own names to be known to God.
And turned now — in heart and in the eyes of the mind,
as before she was turned in body and in the eyes of the flesh —
as soon as she heard herself called by her name,
she knew, as a good sheep, the voice of her shepherd.
Before, turned in body, she thought Jesus to be what he was not;
now, turned in heart, she knew what he was,
and as if coming back to life, adoring him with joy unspeakable, she says: Rabboni.
O changing of the right hand of the Most High:
the great grief is turned into great joy,
the tears of sorrow are changed into tears of love and exultation.
It was not enough for her to see Jesus and to speak with Jesus, unless she also touched him;
for she knew that power went forth from him, and healed all.
But he, willing to sow faith in her heart, says: Do not touch me —
that is, with the hands of the body, whom you have not yet touched with the faith of the heart.
As if the Lord said: I would rather you touch me by the touch of the mind,
believing me equal to the Father,
than by the touch of the body, believing me a mere man and less than the Father.
For still she believed in him carnally, whom she wept for as a man.
She was forbidden, because through a defect of faith she was unworthy.
So Mary, full of doubts, restless with grief,
had need of preparation, before she might draw near to touch so great a holiness.
prayer: But, O sweet Jesus, why do you so keep off from your sacred and most longed-for feet one who loves you?
Do not touch me, you say. Why, O Lord?
And why may I not touch those longed-for footprints of yours,
pierced with nails for me, and bathed in blood?
Why may I not touch them, and kiss them?
Are you more unfriendly than you were wont, because you are more glorious?
Behold, I will not let you go, nor turn back from you;
I will not be kept from tears, and my breast will be broken with sobs and sighs, unless I touch you.
And he says: Do not fear; this good is not taken from you, but deferred.

latinJewel.latin: O mutatio dexteræ Excelsi, conversus est dolor magnus in gaudium magnum, mutatæ sunt lacrymæ doloris in lacrymas amoris et exsultationis.
latinJewel.english: O changing of the right hand of the Most High: the great grief is turned into great joy, the tears of sorrow are changed into tears of love and exultation.
